data.title: Trainual: Document & Train Your Team in Hours, Not Weeks
data.excerpt: What is Trainual? This documentation platform builds SOPs, onboarding flows, and training libraries that actually get used. See why teams love it.

$media.alt: Trainual: Document & Train Your Team in Hours, Not Weeks

children.text: *Disclosure: This post contains affiliate links. If you sign up through them, we may earn a commission at no extra cost to you.*

I have spent years watching founders and ops managers drown in Google Docs, Slack threads, and sticky notes trying to capture "how we do things here." The chaos is real. People ask the same questions over and over. New hires take forever to ramp. And when someone goes on vacation, half the team realizes nobody else knows how to run their processes.

Trainual fixes this. It is a documentation and onboarding platform built specifically for growing teams that need to capture knowledge, create repeatable processes, and train people fast — without turning it into a full-time job.

If you have ever wished for a single place where your entire team could find answers, learn procedures, and track their progress, keep reading. Trainual might be the exact tool you have been looking for.
What Exactly Is Trainual?
Trainual is a cloud-based platform that combines three things most businesses struggle with separately:
process documentation
standard operating procedures (SOPs)
, and
team training

Instead of scattering your company knowledge across dozens of tools, Trainual puts everything into one organized, searchable system. Think of it as a living employee handbook that actually gets updated and actually gets read.

Here is the core idea: you build "subjects" (which are essentially training modules or process guides) inside Trainual. Each subject can include text, images, videos, embeds, and quizzes. You then assign these subjects to specific roles or people. Trainual tracks who has completed what, sends reminders, and gives managers a clear dashboard showing team progress.
The result? Your onboarding goes from "shadow someone for two weeks and hope for the best" to "here is your structured path — go learn and check in when you are done."

I have seen companies cut their onboarding time by 50–70% after switching to Trainual. That is not marketing fluff. When new hires have a clear, step-by-step path with built-in accountability, they ramp faster. Period.
How Trainual Works (The Core Experience)
Getting started with Trainual is refreshingly simple. You sign up, set up your organization structure (departments, roles, teams), and start building content.
The platform revolves around a few key building blocks:
Subjects
: These are your training modules or process documents. Each subject lives inside a category and can be assigned to one or multiple roles.
Steps
: Within each subject, you create step-by-step content. Each step supports rich text, images, video uploads, Loom embeds, and interactive elements.
Tests
: Add quizzes at the end of subjects to verify comprehension. This is huge for compliance-heavy industries or any team that needs to confirm people actually read the material.
Roles
: Define roles in your company and assign subjects to them. When someone is tagged with a role, they automatically see the subjects they need to complete.
People
: Invite your team, assign roles, and track completion. The analytics dashboard shows exactly where everyone stands.
What I love about the authoring experience is how visual and drag-and-drop it feels. You are not fighting with formatting. You build content the way you think — step by step, topic by topic.
Here is a step-by-step walkthrough if you want to see exactly how to set it up

And if you do not want to start from scratch, Trainual offers a library of pre-built templates for common processes: hiring, HR policies, sales procedures, accounting workflows, and more. You can clone a template, customize it for your business, and have a polished SOP ready in under an hour.
One feature that genuinely surprised me:
Trainual's org chart and role clarity tools

. You can map out your entire company structure, define who reports to whom, and clarify exactly what each role is responsible for. This alone eliminates so much confusion in growing teams. When people know who owns what, accountability goes through the roof.
Ready to see how Trainual stacks up against other documentation tools? This comparison breaks it down
New & Highlighted Features
Trainual keeps shipping improvements, and a few recent additions stand out:
AI-Powered Content Generation

— Trainual now includes AI tools that help you draft SOPs and training content faster. You describe a process in plain language, and the AI generates a structured first draft. It is not perfect (you still need to review and customize), but it shaves hours off the initial creation process. For a team that has zero documentation, this is a game-changer.
Enhanced Search & Navigation

— The platform's search has gotten noticeably smarter. Team members can type a question in plain English and land on the exact step or subject that answers it. This is critical because the whole point of documentation is that people can actually find what they need when they need it.
Video and Screen Recording Integration

— You can now record screen captures directly inside Trainual and attach them to steps. No more recording in one tool, uploading to another, and embedding in a third. Record your process walkthrough right where it belongs.
Improved Analytics & Completion Tracking

— The reporting dashboard gives managers granular visibility: who completed what, who is behind, which subjects have the highest drop-off rates, and where people are getting stuck on quizzes. Data-driven training, not guesswork.
Integrations Galore
— Trainual plays nicely with the tools you already use: Slack for notifications, Google Workspace for embedding docs, Loom for video, and payroll/HR systems for automatic onboarding triggers.
Want to explore all the latest features in detail? Check out this deep dive
Who Is Trainual Ideal For?
Trainual is not trying to be everything to everyone. It shines brightest for specific types of teams and businesses:
Growing Small Businesses (5–200 employees)

— This is Trainual's sweet spot. If you are past the startup phase where everyone just knows everything by osmosis, but you are not yet a massive enterprise with a dedicated L&D department, Trainual fills the gap perfectly.
Remote and Distributed Teams

— When your team is not in the same building (or even the same time zone), having a single source of truth for how things work is non-negotiable. Trainual gives remote employees the structure and clarity they need without requiring someone to be online to answer questions in real time.
Franchises and Multi-Location Businesses
— Trainual is genuinely built for franchises. You can create standardized processes that roll out across every location, track compliance, and ensure consistency without micromanaging each site.
Agencies and Service Businesses
— If your team delivers client work and you need consistent quality across projects, documenting your workflows in Trainual means any team member can pick up any project and follow the same process.
Here are some real-world use cases that show this in action
Founders Who Want to Step Back

— I hear this story constantly: a founder is the bottleneck because they are the only one who knows how half the business runs. Trainual is the tool that lets you extract that knowledge from your head, document it, and hand it off so you can focus on strategy instead of operations.
HR and People Ops Teams

— Onboarding is one of the most time-consuming (and error-prone) parts of bringing on new hires. Trainual gives HR teams a repeatable, trackable onboarding system that works whether you are hiring one person or twenty.
Essentially: if your team needs to learn processes, follow procedures, or onboard into roles, Trainual is worth serious consideration.
Why Teams Love Trainual (Real Benefits)
I talk to a lot of tool users, and the feedback on Trainual is remarkably consistent. Here is what keeps coming up:
"We actually use it."

This is the highest compliment any documentation tool can receive. So many teams build elaborate wikis or Notion databases that nobody opens after week two. Trainual's assignment system, completion tracking, and quiz features create accountability that keeps people engaged.
"Onboarding is no longer a nightmare."
New hires get a clear path. Managers get visibility into progress. Nobody has to repeat the same training five times. The structured approach to onboarding saves dozens of hours per hire.
"We stopped losing knowledge when people left."
Employee turnover used to mean losing institutional knowledge. With Trainual, processes are documented and accessible regardless of who is on the team.
"It is easy enough that non-technical people build content."

You do not need to be a tech person or instructional designer. The editor is intuitive enough that department heads and team leads create their own SOPs without waiting for IT or ops to do it for them.
"The org chart alone was worth it."
Multiple users have told me that simply mapping out roles and responsibilities in Trainual revealed gaps and overlaps they did not know existed. Clarity about who does what is powerful.
If you want practical tips on getting the most out of the platform,
this guide covers best practices and workflows that top Trainual users follow
Trainual Pricing: What to Expect

Trainual offers tiered pricing based on the number of users and features you need. Plans start with a base tier for smaller teams and scale up to include advanced features like priority support, custom integrations, and enhanced analytics for larger organizations.
Every plan includes the core functionality: unlimited subjects, role assignments, completion tracking, and access to the template library.
You can explore current pricing and plans right here

What I appreciate about Trainual's pricing model is that it does not gate essential features behind the most expensive plan. Even the entry-level tier gives you a fully functional documentation and training platform.
Getting Started: Your Next Step

If you have read this far, you are probably dealing with at least one of these situations: inconsistent processes, slow onboarding, knowledge silos, or the nagging feeling that your team's tribal knowledge is one resignation letter away from disappearing.
Trainual solves all of these. It is purpose-built for exactly these problems, and it does the job well.
My recommendation: start with the free trial.
Sign up for Trainual here

, pick one process that your team struggles with — maybe your new hire onboarding or your client intake workflow — and document it. See how it feels. Invite two or three team members to complete it. Watch how quickly the lightbulb goes on.
Most teams that try Trainual end up expanding it across their entire organization within a month. It is one of those tools that proves its value fast.
Check out our full comparison of documentation tools if you want to see how Trainual fits into the broader landscape

children.text: What is Trainual used for?

Trainual is a documentation and onboarding platform that helps businesses create, organize, and assign SOPs, training modules, and process guides to their teams. It tracks completion, includes quizzes for comprehension, and serves as a single source of truth for company knowledge.
Is Trainual worth it for small businesses?

Absolutely. Trainual is specifically designed for growing businesses in the 5–200 employee range. It replaces scattered Google Docs, outdated handbooks, and verbal training with a structured, trackable system. Most small businesses see ROI within the first month through faster onboarding and fewer repeated questions.
Can I try Trainual before committing to a paid plan?

Yes. Trainual offers a free trial so you can build content, invite team members, and experience the platform before making a financial commitment. You can sign up and start documenting your first process today to see if it fits your team's needs.
